channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Runnin' Utes get run over by Texas Tech, 93-65
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It may be a long first season in the Big 12 Conference for the Utes basketball team. Darrion Williams scored 19 points with nine assists, Federiko Federiko added 14 points on 7-of-9 shooting — including five dunks — on Saturday, and Texas Tech beat Utah 93-65.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah State holds off Fresno State to improve to 14-1
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Utah State is proving to be the team to beat in the Mountain West Conference this season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Joe Burrow and the Cincinnati Bengals keep their playoff hopes alive by edging the Steelers 19-17
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Joe Burrow will head to Cincinnati's training facility on Sunday. He'll lift weights. He might watch some video. And then he'll join the rest of his teammates and hope. It's not much.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bengals defense steps up to keep slim playoff chances alive in season finale
item.description: The Cincinnati Bengals still have a chance to make the playoffs after their defense stepped up for a 19-17 victory over the Steelers on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US beats Czechia 4-1 to set up world junior hockey final against Finland
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Boston University's Cole Eiserman broke a second-period tie and the defending champion United States beat Czechia 4-1 on Saturday night to advance to the world junior hockey championship game.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Heat begin a new chapter: Life without Jimmy Butler, who is suspended and seeking a trade
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — For the 119th time since Jimmy Butler joined Miami, the Heat played a game without him. This was different from the others. And it went very — very — badly for Miami.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ravens wrap up the AFC North with a 35-10 rout of Cleveland; Jackson surpasses 4,000 yards passing
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The road to a division title was bumpy for the Baltimore Ravens, who started 0-2, faced a schedule packed with playoff teams and had to rally from a two-game deficit with four to play.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Livvy Dunne impresses in LSU's season opener with boyfriend Paul Skenes in attendance
item.description: Livvy Dunne began her quest to become a back-to-back national champion Friday night, and her LSU alum boyfriend Paul Skenes was in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers fined $11,255 ahead of finale of Jets' abysmal season
item.description: The NFL fined quarterback Aaron Rodgers over $11,000 for a late hit against the Bills ahead of the New York Jets' final game of a tumultuous season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man City turns corner with successive wins in Premier League as Arsenal and Chelsea drop points
item.description: Erling Haaland and Manchester City might just be turning the corner after the team's worst run of results under Pep Guardiola.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 1 Tennessee beats No. 23 Arkansas 76-52 to match best start in program history
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Chaz Lanier scored 29 points to lead No. 1 Tennessee over No. 23 Arkansas 76-52 on Saturday and tie for the best start to a season in program history.
item.dc:creator: AL LESAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: School told girls 'transgenders have more rights' as trans runner took away girl's varsity spot, parent says
item.description: A California school district told girls that 'transgenders have more rights than cisgenders,' after a trans athlete took a girls' varsity spot, a parent says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PGA Tour moves toward changes to FedEx Cup finale. Finding a solution everyone likes won't be easy
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Commissioner Jay Monahan wasn't kidding when he told the PGA Tour staff in his year-end message that “everything was on the table.” That includes what could be another massive overhaul at the Tour Championship to determine the FedEx Cup champion.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota beats Virginia Tech 24-10 in Duke's Mayo Bowl for 8th straight bowl victory
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Moments before being doused with a five-gallon tub of mayonnaise, Minnesota coach P.J.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: BBB provides tips on buying Detroit Lions tickets, avoiding scams
item.description: The Better Business Bureau of West Michigan is providing some tips if you’re looking to buy tickets to the Detroit Lions game on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bunchman, NewsNation

item.title: Bill keeping trans athletes out of women's sports will be heard by Congress after new rules package passes
item.description: The new Republican-controlled Congress will hear arguments on a bill that would prohibit transgender athletes from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami Heat suspend star Jimmy Butler for 7 games after he requested trade, said he had no 'joy' with team
item.description: The Miami Heat on Friday announced that they are suspending forward Jimmy Butler and will listen to trade offers for him, likely ending his tenure with the team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charlie Morton agrees to a 1-year, $15 million deal with the Orioles
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Veteran right-hander Charlie Morton and the Baltimore Orioles agreed to a one-year, $15 million contract on Friday. The 41-year-old Morton, the hero of Game 7 of the 2017 World Series with Houston, will try to fill a hole in Baltimore's rotation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PGA Tour contemplating changes to Tour Championship, AP sources say
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — The PGA Tour is contemplating another change to the format at the Tour Championship to crown a FedEx Cup champion as part of its response to what fans, television and corporate partners want to see.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers sign star out of Korean baseball league
item.description: If you thought the Los Angeles Dodgers would slow down on player signings because they already assembled a world champion roster, then you were wrong. According to the team on Friday, the Dodgers have added one of this offseason's most sought-after prizes: Korean star second baseman Hyeseong Kim.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rams QB Matthew Stafford gets home inspected amid string of NFL home burglaries
item.description: A string of high-profile burglaries at the homes of NFL players that has even prompted a warning from the FBI has led one of the brightest stars of Los Angeles to take a proactive approach to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lenny Randle, major leaguer repeatedly in the spotlight for 12 seasons, dies at 75
item.description: MURRIETA, Calif. (AP) — Lenny Randle, a big league player for 12 seasons who spoke five languages, performed stand-up comedy, was dubbed “The Most Interesting Man in Baseball” and was suspended for punching his Texas Rangers manager, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: Blues, Blackhawks close 2024 with the Winter Classic
item.description: It's Winter Classic Day in the Windy City on New Year's Eve. The ultimate outdoor hockey showdown between the cross-state rival St. Louis Blues and Chicago Blackhawks is underway.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: SDFNL All-Star Showcase
item.description:
Boys All-Star Game : Blue Stars 21- Red Stars 17
Girls Flag Football All-Star Game: Blue Stars 6 - Red Stars 0
San Diego 13U Youth Super Bowl: San Diego Bolts 34 - Oceanside Warriors 22
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Thompson-Herro fight leads to ejections of multiple players and coaches in Heat's victory in Houston
item.description: Rockets forward Amen Thompson threw Heat guard Tyler Herro to the floor to trigger an altercation that resulted in six ejections in the closing minute of Miami's 104-100 victory over Houston on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Moment of silence for former President Jimmy Carter held before the Falcons-Commanders game
item.description: Jimmy Carter was honored with a moment of silence before the Atlanta Falcons’ game at the Washington Commanders on Sunday night, hours after the 39th president of the United States died at the age of 100 in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU crushes Colorado in Alamo Bowl, 36-14
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - L.J. Martin rushed for 93 yards and two touchdowns, Jake Retzlaff passed for 151 yards, and No. 17 BYU beat 20th-ranked Colorado 36-14 in the Alamo Bowl on Saturday night. “Understanding that they have great players on the other side of the field," Martin said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
